narrative: Pre-launch operations for the Helios-3 vehicle on LC-2A followed the FAA-approved Part 450 vehicle operator license issued 2025-08-14, with the Flight Safety Analysis demonstrating an expected casualty figure of 7.4e-5, comfortably below the 1e-4 collective threshold. The autonomous flight termination unit completed its qualification campaign per RCC 319-19 and was hot-fired with a two-string vote logic before integration. Hazard area clearance was confirmed by USCG NOTMAR and FAA NOTAM, with the over-flight gate evaluated against the latest 18 SDS conjunction screening. Propellant loading used the certified two-person concurrent verification protocol, and all foreign nationals on console were screened against the approved TAA covering ITAR USML Category XV(a) hardware. Post-mission disposal is planned via controlled deorbit within 18 months, well inside the 25-year LEO rule. Maximum probable loss insurance of $187M is bound and on file with AST.
